title: Benchmark launches EV anode active material price forecasts
gist: Benchmark Mineral Intelligence launched two new EV-specific high-energy anode active material (AAM) price forecasts through 2050, projecting ~5% real price growth for both synthetic and natural graphite grades between Q1 2026 and Q1 2028, while EV anode demand is forecast to more than double from 1.5TWh in 2025 to 3.2TWh in 2030 and graphite retains 91% of anode demand share through 2030 despite silicon-anode development.

insights.claim: Benchmark Mineral Intelligence expanded its Graphite & Anode Service with two new EV-specific anode active material price forecasts — Synthetic Graphite Anode High Energy and Natural Graphite Anode High Energy (both DDP China, ≥99.97% C) — forecast through to 2050.
insights.speaker: Benchmark Team
insights.so_what: Gives procurement and investment teams long-term, grade-specific price visibility across the full anode material spectrum for contract negotiation and capex planning.
insights.quote: Synthetic Graphite Anode High Energy, DDP China, ≥99.97% C, 350–370 mAh/g (>2.5C)Natural Graphite Anode High Energy, DDP China, ≥99.97% C, 360–370 mAh/g (>2.5C)

insights.claim: Differentiated price visibility across anode material grades is becoming essential as the battery supply chain specializes, according to Benchmark's graphite and anode research manager Tony Alderson.
insights.speaker: Tony Alderson
insights.so_what: Signals that generic (blended) graphite pricing is losing relevance for investors and buyers as grade-level specialization increases margin dispersion across producers.
insights.quote: As the battery supply chain becomes more specialised, differentiated price visibility across anode material grades is essential

insights.claim: Benchmark forecasts that both natural and synthetic graphite EV anode active material prices will rise around 5% between Q1 2026 and Q1 2028 on a real (2026) basis.
insights.speaker: Benchmark Team
insights.so_what: A modest but positive real price trajectory for graphite anode materials over the next two years supports upstream graphite miner and anode processor margins after a period of price weakness.
insights.quote: Benchmark forecasts that between Q1 2026 and Q1 2028 natural and synthetic graphite EV AAM material prices will rise around 5%, respectively, on a real (2026) basis.

insights.claim: The battery energy storage systems (BESS) sector is growing anode demand 34% year-on-year between 2025 and 2026, but electric vehicles are expected to remain the dominant source of anode demand throughout Benchmark's assessment period.
insights.speaker: Benchmark Team
insights.so_what: BESS is a fast-growing secondary demand pool for graphite anode, but EV demand remains the primary volume driver investors should track for anode market sizing.
insights.quote: the battery energy stationary storage (BESS) sector is recording robust year-on-year growth of 34% between 2025 and 2026, EVs are expected to remain the dominant source of anode demand
insights.locator: How large is the EV anode market?

insights.claim: EV anode material demand, measured from cell supply, is forecast to more than double from 1.5TWh in 2025 to 3.2TWh in 2030, per Benchmark's Q2 2026 report.
insights.speaker: Benchmark Team
insights.so_what: A greater-than-2x demand growth trajectory over five years is a core volume assumption for anyone modeling graphite anode capacity build-out and capex needs.
insights.quote: EV anode material demand, measured from cell supply, is forecast to more than double from 1.5TWh in 2025 to 3.2TWh in 2030.
insights.locator: How large is the EV anode market?

insights.claim: Synthetic and natural graphite are forecast to together account for 91% of global anode material demand by 2030, meaning silicon-based and other emerging anode materials will remain a minority of the market despite rapid development.
insights.speaker: Benchmark Team
insights.so_what: Tempers the silicon-anode disruption narrative: graphite remains the dominant investable exposure for anode-material liquidity and traded volume through at least 2030.
insights.quote: traditional anode material routes, which are forecast to account for 91% of global anode material demand by 2030
